name: ExtensionIngredient_For
title: R5: The product which this ingredient is a constituent part of (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Ingredient.for` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Ingredient.for` 0..* `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/MedicinalProductDefinition,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/AdministrableProductDefinition,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ManufacturedItemDefinition)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Ingredient.for` 0..* `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/MedicinalProductDefinition,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/AdministrableProductDefinition,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ManufacturedItemDefinition)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Ingredient.for` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `Ingredient` is not mapped.
Element `Ingredient.for` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
